title: Agrostis leersii
taxonomy: Agrostis leersii is classified within the family Poaceae, which is commonly referred to as the grass family. This classification places it among the monocots, a group of flowering plants that have one cotyledon in their seeds. The genus Agrostis is a group of grasses, many of which are found in temperate regions around the world. A. leersii is further classified within the subfamily Pooideae, which includes many temperate grasses. Its specific classification helps scientists understand its evolutionary relationships and characteristics.

intro: Agrostis leersii, commonly known as Leers' bent or Leers' red-stemmed bentgrass, is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of respiratory issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Agrostis leersii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Agrostis leersii is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also employed in traditional medicine to treat a range of health conditions.
